title_tesim: Women of Juliaca Peru
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Richard and Dion Neutra Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA
description_tesim: Group of ten women and children wearing traditional Peruvian dress (including serape's) standing in various positions in Juliaca, Peru.  Possibly at the Bolivian border.   Accoring to Koch's Mench und Wohnen the drawing is of a Peruvian village in the mountains.  Vast market place under giant trees and in front of a small church.
Inscription by artist reads "Women of Juliaca Peru." Signed and dated on bottom left.
